prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens in a dim, greenish interior. A person is bent forward near a wall or work area; a small step stool or ladder-like object is visible at the left. The image is blurry, so the exact activity is unclear.

The scene cuts to a close-up of a woman with light-colored hair wearing a patterned headscarf and a plaid shirt. She holds up an aerosol can toward the camera. The can has large block lettering, including “BIG” near the top and “WALL” near the bottom; the full product name is partly obscured by blur and glare.

Next, the woman is shown in profile from above or behind, facing a wall. She appears to be spraying the wall with the aerosol can. A visible spray or mist comes from the can toward the wall surface.

The following wider shot shows the woman standing at the wall, arm raised as if continuing to spray or wipe a spot on the wall. A small stool is nearby. A man in a fitted white T-shirt stands to the left, watching her. The wall behind them appears plain and greenish, with a lighter area where she is working.

Near the end, the man is shown in a frontal medium close-up holding the same aerosol can beside his chest. Behind him is the wall with a pale, human-shaped clean patch or silhouette. On-screen text appears over his shirt area reading: “Lifts the dirt right off the wall.” The can again shows bold lettering, including “BIG” and “WALL.”

The final sampled frame is black, indicating a fade-out or cut to black.
